title: Pressure area risk review

assessment.text: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult section supports interval mobility, prior or current pressure injury, nutrition, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour, heat, firmness, moisture and pain when assessed.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — adult risk and skin assessment

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact ulcer documentation section supports clinician-measured area, depth, undermining, photography and category when actually completed; generic plan fields remain clinician-entered.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 and 1.5.1–1.5.3 — ulcer measurement and categorisation

sources_used.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Exact skin site and findings, validated risk tool, category, wound measurement, infection, equipment, treatment, and monitoring remain unsupported unless entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
